#include <skl_mpg4.h>

void Skl_MP4_Delete_Decoder (SKL_MP4_DEC *)

Detailed Description

SKL_MP4_DEC is the main decoding class.

All methods are virtual, just like the COM-object paradigm. It eases the dynamic class loading (just map the proxy's symbol from dll, the vtbl will come along).

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

SKL_MP4_DEC::SKL_MP4_DEC (   )  [protected]
 

protected constructor. use Skl_MP4_New_Decoder()

virtual SKL_MP4_DEC::~SKL_MP4_DEC (   )  [protected, virtual]
 

protected destructor. use Skl_MP4_Delete_Decoder()

Member Function Documentation

virtual void SKL_MP4_DEC::Consume_Frame (  SKL_MP4_PIC *  Pic  )  [pure virtual]
 

Definitively retreive the last decoded frame. The data will remain valid until the next call to Decode() or Decode_MPEG12(). Warning : beware of the SKL_MP4_PIC data layout!

Parameters:
Pic the structure to be filled with last frame's data.
See also:
Decode

SKL_MP4_PIC

virtual int SKL_MP4_DEC::Decode (  const SKL_BYTE *  Buf,
int  Len
)  [pure virtual]
 

This function decodes 'Len' bytes of bitstream pointed to by 'Buf'. It returns the number of bytes consumed from the bitstream. For safety, it is required that the buffer contains at least a full chunk of VOP data (start code 0x000001b6) within it boundary. This is to ensure complete parsing of syntax elements.

If the 'Len' argument is 0, the last decoded frame will be removed from the decoder and made available for Consume_Frame(). Keep in mind that the decoder has a latency of one frame, so that inbetween incoming B-VOP can be decoded using future prediction. Hence, call with Len=0 should be performed at the end of the bitstream decoding, when you are sure that no frame are left to decode.

Parameters:
Buf the bitstream's bytes
Len the number of bytes available. If equal to zero, the very last residual frame, will be made available.

virtual int SKL_MP4_DEC::Decode_MPEG12 (  const SKL_BYTE *  Buf,
int  Len
)  [pure virtual]
 

This function is the equivalent of Decode(), for partial MPEG1/2 decoding function. It only supports FRAME pic struct/4:2:0 format. You must call it when you are sure the bitstream is a MPEG1/2 one (no autodetection is available).

See also:
Decode

virtual int SKL_MP4_DEC::Get_Frame_Number (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns the current frame's number (if available).

See also:
Is_Frame_Ready

Decode

virtual int SKL_MP4_DEC::Is_Frame_Ready (   )  const [pure virtual]
 

Returns true if the current frame is finished decoding

See also:
Decode

virtual void SKL_MP4_DEC::Set_CPU (  SKL_CPU_FEATURE  Cpu = SKL_CPU_DETECT  )  [pure virtual]
 

Sets the CPU feature to use.

Parameters:
Cpu : see the enum description for a list of available cpu features.

virtual SKL_MEM_I* SKL_MP4_DEC::Set_Memory_Manager (  SKL_MEM_I *  Mem = 0  )  [pure virtual]
 

Set a new memory pool to use internally for subsequent memory allocation. Warning: it should preferably be called at start-up only, to ensure that cycles of New()/Delete() are performed on the same memory pool.

Parameters:
Mem The memory pool to use. If equal to 0 (default), the C++ heap will be used.
Returns:
This function will return the previous memory manager.
See also:
SKL_MEM_I

virtual void SKL_MP4_DEC::Set_Slicer (  SKL_MP4_SLICER  Slicer,
SKL_ANY  Slicer_Data = 0
)  [pure virtual]
 

Set post-decoding hook function. @ see SKL_MP4_SLICER

Parameters:
Slicer callback function to use
Slicer_Data can be anything, and will be passed as is to the Slicer hook function.
